title: lichen_l2.c:464 peer_find_oldest_locked INT64_MAX comparison with k_uptime_get
description:
Completeness check: Round 1-2 missed potential k_uptime_get overflow timing issue.

WHAT: peer_find_oldest_locked() compares peer_table[i].last_seen (int64_t) against INT64_MAX to find the oldest peer for LRU eviction. The last_seen field is set via k_uptime_get() which returns int64_t milliseconds since boot.

WHY BUG: On a system running for ~292 million years, k_uptime_get() would overflow int64_t. This is practically impossible, BUT the comparison uses INT64_MAX as a sentinel value. If a peer's last_seen were somehow set to INT64_MAX (via bug or corruption), it would NEVER be evicted since it would appear to be the 'newest' peer forever. This violates the LRU invariant.

IMPACT: Theoretical only - k_uptime_get() cannot return INT64_MAX during normal operation. However, defensive coding would use a different sentinel or skip checking last_seen if it equals INT64_MAX.

FIX: Either:
1. Use -1 as sentinel (impossible from k_uptime_get which starts at 0)
2. Add guard: if (peer_table[i].last_seen == INT64_MAX) continue;
3. Document why INT64_MAX is safe (it is, practically)
